lines.english: So take such medicine, O fool,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਤ ਨਿਤ ਲੇਹੁ ਨ ਛੀਜੈ ਦੇਹ ॥
lines.translit: nit nit lehu na chheejai deh |
lines.english: Take it each and every day, and your body shall not waste away.
At the very last instant, you shall strike down the Messenger of Death. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਖਾਧੈ ਤੇਰੇ ਜਾਹਿ ਵਿਕਾਰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jit khaadhai tere jaeh vikaar |1| rahaau |
lines.english: by which your corruption shall be taken away. ||1||Pause||
as are the vehicles you see moving around.
Power, wealth and youth are all just shadows,
In the next world it is day, while here, it is all night. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਹ ਨ ਨਾਉ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਜਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: deh na naau na hovai jaat |
lines.english: Neither your body, nor your fame, nor your social status shall go along with you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਦ ਕਰਿ ਸਮਧਾਂ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਘਿਉ ਤੇਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: saad kar samadhaan trisanaa ghiau tel |
lines.english: Let your taste for pleasures be the firewood, let your greed be the ghee,
and your sexual desire and anger the cooking oil; burn them in the fire.
Whatever pleases God is acceptable. ||3||
Some make burnt offerings, hold sacred feasts, and read the Puraanas.
Those for whom this treasure is ordered,
look wealthy when they reach their true home.
Intense meditation is the paper, and Your Name is the insignia.
O Nanak, blessed is that mother who gave birth to them. ||4||3||8||
Your nose is sharp, and your eyes are black.
You wear white clothes, and speak sweet words.
Malaar, First Mehl:
By Your power, I fly and soar, and ascend to the heavens.
Have you ever seen your Lord and Master, O sister? ||1||
O my All-powerful Lord and Master,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਤਨੁ ਸਾਜਿ ਦੀਏ ਨਾਲਿ ਖੰਭ ॥
lines.translit: jin tan saaj dee naal khanbh |
lines.english: He fashioned the body, and gave it wings;
I see Him in the water, on the land, in the mountains, on the river-banks,
in all places and interspaces, O brother. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨ ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਜਾਇਗਾ ਨ ਜਾਹਿਗੇ ਖੰਭ ॥
lines.translit: n ihu tan jaaeigaa na jaahige khanbh |
lines.english: Neither this body, nor its wings, shall go to the world hereafter.
When He bestows His Glance of Grace, I am comforted and consoled.
He gave it great thirst and desire to fly.
As He makes me see, so do I see, O brother. ||3||
It is a fusion of air, water and fire.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕਰਮੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਜਪੀਐ ਕਰਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਪੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak karam hovai japeeai kar gur peer |
lines.english: O Nanak, if it is in the mortal's karma, then he meditates on the Lord, with the Guru as his Spiritual Teacher.
This body is absorbed in the Truth. ||4||4||9||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Malaar, Third Mehl, Chau-Padhay, First House:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੁ ਆਕਾਰੁ ਹੈ ਆਪੇ ਆਪੇ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: nirankaar aakaar hai aape aape bharam bhulaae |
lines.english: The Formless Lord is formed by Himself. He Himself deludes in doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਤਾ ਆਪੇ ਵੇਖੈ ਜਿਤੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਤੁ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: kar kar karataa aape vekhai jit bhaavai tith laae |
lines.english: Creating the Creation, the Creator Himself beholds it; He enjoins us as He pleases.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣਾ ਭਾਣਾ ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਗੁਰ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਲਹੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: aapanaa bhaanaa aape jaanai gur kirapaa te laheeai |
lines.english: Only He Himself knows His Will. By Guru's Grace, it is grasped.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਵਕ ਕਉ ਏਹਾ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਜਾ ਕਉ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਮਨਾਏ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sevak kau ehaa vaddiaaee jaa kau hukam manaae |1|
lines.english: This is the true greatness of His servant, that he obeys the Hukam of the Lord's Command. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹਾ ਸਕਤਿ ਸਿਵੈ ਘਰਿ ਆਵੈ ਜੀਵਦਿਆ ਮਰਿ ਰਹੀਐ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ehaa sakat sivai ghar aavai jeevadiaa mar raheeai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: When this play of Shiva and Shakti comes to his home, he remains dead while yet alive. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੇਦ ਪੜੈ ਪੜਿ ਵਾਦੁ ਵਖਾਣੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਮਹੇਸਾ ॥
lines.translit: ved parrai parr vaad vakhaanai brahamaa bisan mahesaa |
lines.english: They read the Vedas, and read them again, and engage in arguments about Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹ ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਣ ਮਾਇਆ ਜਿਨਿ ਜਗਤੁ ਭੁਲਾਇਆ ਜਨਮ ਮਰਣ ਕਾ ਸਹਸਾ ॥
lines.translit: eeh trigun maaeaa jin jagat bhulaaeaa janam maran kaa sahasaa |
lines.english: This three-phased Maya has deluded the whole world into cynicism about death and birth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਏਕੋ ਜਾਣੈ ਚੂਕੈ ਮਨਹੁ ਅੰਦੇਸਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee eko jaanai chookai manahu andesaa |2|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, know the One Lord, and the anxiety of your mind will be allayed. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਦੀਨ ਮੂਰਖ ਅਵੀਚਾਰੀ ਤੁਮ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਕਰਹੁ ਹਮਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: ham deen moorakh aveechaaree tum chintaa karahu hamaaree |
lines.english: I am meek, foolish and thoughtless, but still, You take care of me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੁ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਦੇਹਿ ਤੂ ਅਪਣਾ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਖਾਣੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: ek nidhaan dehi too apanaa ahinis naam vakhaanee |3|
lines.english: Please bless me with the treasure of the One Name, that I may chant it, day and night. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਹੁ ਦਇਆਲ ਕਰਿ ਦਾਸੁ ਦਾਸਾ ਕਾ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੀ ਤੁਮਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: hohu deaal kar daas daasaa kaa sevaa karee tumaaree |
lines.english: Please be kind to me, and make me the slave of Your slaves, so that I may serve You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਜਲ ਊਪਰਿ ਫੇਨੁ ਬੁਦਬੁਦਾ ਤੈਸਾ ਇਹੁ ਸੰਸਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: jiau jal aoopar fen budabudaa taisaa ihu sansaaraa |
lines.english: Like foam bubbling up on the surface of the water, so is this world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਬੂਝਹੁ ਕੋਈ ਐਸਾ ਕਰੇ ਵੀਚਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kehat naanak gur parasaadee boojhahu koee aisaa kare veechaaraa |
lines.english: Says Nanak, by Guru's Grace, understand. Hardly anyone considers this.
